title: ajuga vulgaris subsp. foliosa
intro: Ajuga vulgaris subsp. foliosa, also known as Bugleweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The health benefits of Bugleweed include its use in treating anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Bugleweed is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusement, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as ointments and salves, to treat various skin conditions and wounds.
